profiles.trustBoundary: Static graph artifacts may provide advisory Workspace graph evidence; live Graphify, MCP, watch mode, hooks, cache, and regeneration are not verifier inputs.
profiles.repairHint: Keep declared capability fields, graph evidence docs, fixtures, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex manual executor readiness is human-mediated/manual and not a guarantee that a runnable local Codex CLI exists; local config, slash commands, hooks, plugins, goals, subagents, and app-server availability are not verifier inputs.
profiles.repairHint: Keep Executor Contract docs, Capability Request fixtures, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex config may describe operator affordances such as sandboxing, approvals, apps, MCP servers, skills, hooks, and multi-agent tools; config is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Codex config references, Workspace boundary docs, and registry tests before changing this profile.

profiles.trustBoundary: Codex app-server APIs can describe threads, goals, tools, skills, plugins, MCP servers, and config state for an operator; app-server state is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Codex app-server references and keep any Workspace use framed as manual evidence recording only.

profiles.trustBoundary: Playwright CLI can provide browser automation evidence for a manual Codex operator, but wrapper output, screenshots, traces, dependency resolution, and live browser state are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Playwright wrapper and npx were present during planning.
Wrapper help failed because @playwright/cli resolved an unavailable alpha dependency.

profiles.repairHint: Refresh the Playwright CLI wrapper dependency or pin a resolvable package before changing the unavailable alpha dependency observation.

profiles.trustBoundary: Agent Browser CLI can provide browser snapshots, screenshots, and interaction transcripts for a manual Codex operator, but command presence, daemon state, browser output, and local PATH checks are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: agent-browser command was not detected on PATH during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Install or expose the Agent Browser CLI, rerun PATH help evidence, and keep not detected on PATH recorded until command evidence exists.

profiles.trustBoundary: Browser Use IAB can expose in-app browser navigation, snapshots, screenshots, and interaction context for a manual Codex operator, but plugin availability, node_repl state, page state, and browser-client output are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Browser Use plugin context and skill instructions were available during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Record explicit Browser Use IAB manual evidence before citing browser output; plugin context alone is advisory only.

profiles.trustBoundary: Computer Use MCP can expose desktop app inventory and UI automation context for a manual Codex operator, but app state, accessibility output, user interface actions, and MCP availability are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

observedEvidence.message: Computer Use MCP list_apps responded during planning.

profiles.repairHint: Treat list_apps as app-inventory evidence only; record separate manual evidence before claiming broader desktop action support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Google Calendar remote MCP can expose operator-granted Calendar event tools, but live MCP server discovery, OAuth state, Codex connector availability, target repo manifests, Calendar API enablement, trigger install, deployment state, and Codex config are not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Refresh official Google Calendar MCP docs, source register, fixture, capability contract, and tests before changing support claims.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify query, path, and explain commands may help an operator navigate committed graph artifacts; command output is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

commandEvidence.boundary: Informational operator evidence only; not verifier input, not support promotion, and not grant authority.
commandEvidence.graphFormat: Graphify CLI smoke tests use Graphify-native node-link JSON with nodes[] and links[]; BMAD normalized graph artifacts use nodes[] and edges[] and are not CLI smoke fixtures.

smokeTests.command: uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify query "capability evidence" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json
uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify explain "Capability Evidence" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json
uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify path "Capability Evidence" "Workspace Verifier" --graph test/fixtures/graphify/native-node-link.graph.json

profiles.repairHint: Keep Graphify command references, graph evidence docs, and Workspace tests aligned before claiming query-profile support.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify MCP can expose structured graph access for an operator, but live MCP activation is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.
profiles.repairHint: Keep MCP references advisory, and require explicit manual evidence before any Workspace result mentions live graph-tool output.

profiles.trustBoundary: Graphify hooks, watch mode, cache refresh, and regeneration may explain graph freshness risk; automation output is not verifier input and not Workspace authority.

commandEvidence.boundary: Informational operator evidence only; not verifier input, not support promotion, and not grant authority.

smokeTests.command: uv tool run --from graphifyy graphify hook status

profiles.repairHint: Promote only after committed Workspace evidence proves explicit, bounded hook or regeneration handling without hidden execution.
